summary: Every scrape command (airbnb_listing search/get, cheapest, compare, plan, watch check) persists what it fetched into the local store, best-effort: UpsertAirbnbListing + UpsertHostRecord for the listing and host, and InsertPriceSnapshot only when a real positive price came back. Persistence is a side-effect that must never alter the live result or surface an error.
reason: The scrape commands printed typed results but persisted nothing, so the store-backed readers (host portfolio, wishlist diff) and InsertPriceSnapshot/UpsertHostRecord stayed orphaned with zero callers. Airbnb SSR sometimes returns cards with no stable id (UpsertAirbnbListing rejects empty id) and frequently no bookable total for the given dates (total -> 0). A regen must keep persistence best-effort (swallow store-open/write errors, skip empty-id cards) and keep the price>0 snapshot guard so an unavailable price never becomes a phantom $0 row polluting price history.
validated_outcome: go build/vet green; persist_test asserts a listing row lands, an empty-id card is skipped without error, and a price snapshot is written only for a positive total.
